description: A reddish-brown metal that conducts electricity and develops green patina; the first worked metal. 'Roena' names what glows warm—the friendly metal that ages beautifully.
sound_symbolism: The word glows and ages. 'Ro' radiates with warm reddish glow, copper fresh. The vowel 'e' opens to air. Then 'na' settles into green patina over time.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'roena' (copper). The first metal worked by humans; it conducts and develops green patina. Related to 'keluo' (metal) as its warm-hued kind.

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Patina as beauty—copper's green aging celebrated in Art Nouveau architecture.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Copper was first—humanity's first metal, shaped before smelting was known.
